product_summary: The Pack Product (MTO) agent automates the final stages of order fulfillment by handling packaging, labeling, and sealing of make-to-order products. It verifies items via barcode, applies customer-specific materials, and ensures compliance with hazardous goods and GxP regulations where applicable. This reduces shipping errors, accelerates delivery times, and maintains high standards of accuracy and regulatory adherence, delivering significant operational efficiency and customer satisfaction for executives overseeing supply chain processes.
value_proposition: Automates end-to-end MTO packing with built-in verification and compliance to eliminate shipment errors and delays.
technical_summary: The hybrid agent combines rule-based engines with AI modules to integrate barcode scanning, ERP data feeds, and regulatory databases for real-time mismatch detection and label generation. It enforces compliance layers for dangerous goods, GxP, and GDPR while interfacing with packaging hardware and document systems via standardized APIs.
integration_guide:
# Integration Guide — mto_packing_agent
**Process:** Pack Product (MTO)
**Version:** 1.0.0

## Prerequisites
- Python 3.10+
- Agentic Zero runtime installed
- API credentials configured in .env

## Installation
```bash
# Copy agent to your project
cp mto_packing_agent.py ./agents/
```

## Basic Usage
```python
from agents.mto_packing_agent import MtoPackingAgentAgent

agent = MtoPackingAgentAgent()
result = agent.execute({
    "picked_products": your_picked_products_data,
    "packing_specifications": your_packing_specifications_data,
    "labels": your_labels_data,
})
print(result['outputs'])
```

## Supported Systems
- SAP ECC
- SAP S/4HANA
- SAP EWM
- Oracle ERP Cloud
- Oracle JDE

## Tools Required
- barcode_scanner
- wms_api
- erp_system
- compliance_database
- label_printer
- staging_queue_listener

## Escalation
The agent automatically escalates to human when:
- quantity mismatch or damaged item triggers inventory reconciliation/quarantine
- cycle_time exceeds threshold notifies supervisor
- missing label data escalates to order management
api_endpoints: POST /api/v1/agents/mto_packing_agent/execute — Execute the agent with inputs
GET  /api/v1/agents/mto_packing_agent/status — Get agent status and metrics
demo_script:
1. Open dashboard and load sample MTO order; highlight one-click launch of mto_packing_agent (€699 annual cost displayed).  
2. Run AI packing sequence; show automated material selection, weight validation, and label generation while flagging two compliance rules (hazardous-goods separation, EU packaging regs).  
3. Display real-time audit trail and digital sign-off; confirm 100 % traceability for regulatory review.  
4. Reveal metrics panel: 14 min → 3 min per order, 92 % error reduction, equating to 47 hours/week saved.  
5. Calculate live ROI: €5,600 monthly labor & compliance savings yields 8.0x multiplier on €699 price within first month.  
6. Compare side-by-side manual vs. agent run; note zero compliance deviations and instant report export for executives.  
7. Close with one-page ROI summary and next-step pilot offer.
use_cases: Use case 1: Automotive MTO battery pack assembly uses the agent to auto-validate UN dangerous-goods packaging specs against each custom order, cutting non-compliance shipments from 12% to <1% and eliminating $180K annual recall/fine exposure.

Use case 2: Industrial equipment manufacturer deploys the agent to generate customer-specific crating instructions for made-to-order CNC machines, reducing average packing labor from 4.2 to 2.9 hours per unit and increasing monthly throughput by 18 units.

Use case 3: Multi-site distribution center runs the agent on every MTO shipment label to enforce GDPR data-minimization rules, eliminating manual redaction errors and avoiding potential €2.4M regulatory penalties while cutting label-prep time by 22%.

Use case 4: Heavy machinery OEM uses the agent to optimize dunnage and void-fill calculations for each made-to-order order, lowering packaging material spend by 27% ($312K/year) and reducing dimensional-weight shipping surcharges by 15%.

Use case 5: Automotive Tier-1 supplier integrates the agent to cross-check customer packaging standards against BOM changes in real time, reducing packing-related line-stoppages from 47 to 6 per quarter and recovering $95K in expedited freight costs.
